body:
## Summary
- Add explicit `update-agent` guardrail to merge generated agent ignore rules into the target `.gitignore`
- Add the same instruction to the conservative update workflow step

## Verification
- `git diff --check -- .skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`
- `rg -n "Merge agent-generated-output|Preserve target-owned root files|agent ignore entries|agent/node_modules|agent/dist" .skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`

Closes #28

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and opened PR #55 for issue #28; an automated review is the appropriate next step before merge.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR changes only `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md` with 6 added lines, directly addressing the requested missing `.gitignore` merge guidance. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The fix is plain skill text added in the two relevant sections, with no new machinery or unrelated restructuring. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The added wording mirrors the existing `install-agent` guidance for merging `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/` into target `.gitignore`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR body closes issue #28, whose discussion explicitly narrowed the remaining work to the `update-agent` skill lines cited by the requester. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The user-facing `update-agent` skill now documents the update behavior that was already documented for install-side guidance. |

## Notes

- The implementation satisfies the minimal requested scope: it adds explicit instructions to merge `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/` into the target `.gitignore`, without touching unrelated install docs, root `.gitignore`, tests, or runtime code.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found. Process-oriented selected rubrics about delegated route authorization, preview contracts, workflow stops, merge strategy, and follow-up orchestration were not scored because this PR only changes documentation text in `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #55 minimally updates `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md` for issue #28. The issue’s remaining gap was that `update-agent` did not explicitly tell agents to merge generated-output ignore rules into a target repository’s existing `.gitignore`. The PR adds guidance for `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/` in both the guardrails and conservative update workflow. This matches the already-fixed install-side behavior.

## Review
Both independent reviews found no actionable issues. The change is narrow, documented in the right skill file, and aligned with the linked issue scope.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Reviewers found the PR ready as-is; no branch changes are required. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no violations.
- Codex review reported no findings and a SHIP verdict.
- Existing PR discussion already acknowledges this as the minimal follow-up for issue #28.
- No inline comments were posted because there are no concrete line-specific findings.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: No unresolved actionable work remains for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [x] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #55.

no rubric changes

PR #55’s conversation contained only bot-authored PR comments/reviews. The linked issue #28 had human comments from `lolipopshock`, but GitHub metadata showed `authorAssociation: CONTRIBUTOR`, and the comments asked for a minimal fix rather than an explicit durable rubric update.

The reusable lessons were already covered by active rubrics: `surgical-changes`, `read-docs-first`, and `keep-docs-in-sync`. The rubrics checkout remains unchanged.

title: Add update-agent gitignore merge guidance
